title: Describing the shape of a distribution
problem: A frequency histogram shows the number of hours students spend on homework each week. The bars are tallest on the left (around 1–3 hours) and become shorter towards the right, with a few students recording 8–10 hours. Describe the shape of this distribution and explain what it means in context.

steps.instruction: Identify where most of the data is concentrated.
steps.working: The tallest bars are on the left side of the histogram, around $1$–$3$ hours. This is where most students' data lies.

steps.instruction: Identify the direction of the tail.
steps.working: The bars gradually decrease in height as we move to the right, with a few students in the $8$–$10$ hour range. The tail extends to the right.

steps.instruction: Name the shape of the distribution.
steps.working: Because most data is on the left and the tail extends to the right, this distribution is positively skewed (right-skewed).

In context, most students spend only $1$–$3$ hours on homework each week, while a small number spend considerably more time. The mean will be pulled to the right of the median because of these higher values.

answer: The distribution is positively skewed. Most students spend $1$–$3$ hours on homework, with a few students spending much more time, creating a tail on the right.

title: Identifying and explaining an outlier
problem:
The monthly salaries (in dollars) of eight employees at a small business are:
$$12\,000,\; 12\,500,\; 13\,000,\; 13\,500,\; 14\,000,\; 14\,500,\; 15\,000,\; 72\,000$$
Identify the outlier, then compare the mean salary with and without the outlier. Explain what effect the outlier has.

steps.working: The salaries range from $\$12\,000$ to $\$15\,000$, except for the last value of $\$72\,000$, which is far above the others. The outlier is $\$72\,000$ (likely the owner's salary).

steps.instruction: Calculate the mean of all eight values.
Calculate the mean without the outlier.

steps.working: With the outlier, the mean is $\$20\,812.50$. Without it, the mean is $\$13\,500$. The outlier raises the mean by over $\$7\,000$, making it a poor representation of what a typical employee earns. The median of the seven non-outlier values is $\$13\,500$, which better represents the typical salary.

answer: The outlier is $\$72\,000$. The mean with the outlier is $\$20\,812.50$; without it the mean is $\$13\,500$. The outlier inflates the mean by over $\$7\,000$, making the median a more appropriate measure of centre.

title: Comparing two datasets using centre and spread
problem:
Two brands of batteries were tested for their lifetimes (in hours). The results are summarised below:

$$\begin{array}{lcc} & \text{Brand X} & \text{Brand Y} \\ \hline \text{Mean} & 55 & 52 \\ \text{Median} & 56 & 52 \\ \text{IQR} & 4 & 16 \\ \text{Range} & 12 & 40 \end{array}$$

Compare the two brands and make a recommendation for a customer who wants reliable batteries.

steps.working: Brand X has a mean of $55$ hours and Brand Y has a mean of $52$ hours. Brand X lasts longer on average by $3$ hours. The medians ($56$ vs $52$) also confirm Brand X performs better at the centre of the distribution.

Brand X has an IQR of $4$ hours compared to Brand Y's IQR of $16$ hours. Brand X also has a much smaller range ($12$ vs $40$ hours). The smaller spread for Brand X means its battery lifetimes are much more consistent.

steps.instruction: Note the difference between mean and median for each brand.
steps.working: For Brand X, mean ($55$) and median ($56$) are very close — the distribution is roughly symmetric. For Brand Y, mean and median are both $52$ — also symmetric, but with much greater spread.

Brand X lasts longer on average and is far more consistent (smaller IQR and range). A customer wanting reliable batteries should choose Brand X, as they can expect most batteries to last close to $55$–$56$ hours with little variation.

answer: Brand X lasts longer on average (mean $55$ h vs $52$ h) and is more consistent (IQR $4$ h vs $16$ h). Brand X is the better choice for reliability.
